evals.description: Trigger test: activates on EDA question
evals.prompt: I just got a new CSV dataset with 50 columns and 200k rows. Where do I even start? I want to understand what's in it before doing anything else.

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on hypothesis testing question
evals.prompt: I want to know if users who see a red button convert more than users who see a blue button. How do I test this statistically?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on A/B test analysis
evals.prompt: Our A/B test ran for two weeks. Control had 12,400 users and 620 conversions. Treatment had 12,600 users and 693 conversions. Is this statistically significant?

evals.description: Core task: data cleaning pipeline with missing values
evals.prompt: My DataFrame has 30% missing values in some columns, inconsistent column names with spaces, and duplicates. Give me a clean, reusable function to fix all of this.

evals.description: Core task: choosing the right statistical test
evals.prompt: I have three groups (A, B, C) and a continuous outcome variable. I want to know if the means are different across groups. What test should I run?

evals.description: Core task: feature engineering without data leakage
evals.prompt: I'm building a churn prediction model. I have a date column, some categorical features, and numeric features. Walk me through feature engineering correctly.

evals.description: Core task: visualizing distributions and correlations
evals.prompt: I want to visualize the distribution of my numeric features and see which ones are correlated. Give me clean, production-quality matplotlib/seaborn code.

evals.description: Core task: interpret linear regression coefficients
evals.prompt: I ran an OLS regression and got a coefficient of 2.3 for 'years_experience' with p=0.001 and a coefficient of -0.5 for 'remote_work' with p=0.38. What does this mean?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about p-hacking / peeking at A/B test
evals.prompt: Our A/B test has been running for 3 days and the p-value just hit 0.04. Should I call it a win and stop the test now to ship the feature?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about data leakage from fitting on full dataset
evals.prompt: I scaled all my features using StandardScaler, then split into train and test, and got 98% accuracy. My manager is excited but I'm not sure this is real. Am I missing something?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about correlation vs causation
evals.prompt: I found that ice cream sales are strongly correlated (r=0.92) with drowning deaths. Does this mean ice cream causes drowning? What should I conclude?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend using mean for skewed salary data
evals.prompt: Our dataset has salary values ranging from $30k to $2M with a very long right tail. I want to report the 'typical' salary. Should I use the mean?
